Most changes to Python [require a NEWS entry](https://devguide.python.org/core-developers/committing/#updating-news-and-what-s-new-in-python). Add one using the [blurb_it](https://blurb-it.herokuapp.com) web app or the [blurb](https://pypi.org/project/blurb/) command-line tool.

If this change has little impact on Python users, wait for a maintainer to apply the `skip news` label instead.

comments.author: ritvikpasham
comments.body:
> I don't think we need a whole new file for the test, as it's only a 3 line reproducer. You could just add it alongside the existing tests for memoryview iteration.

Yeah, that makes sense! Would you happen to know where the memoryview iteration tests are?

comments.author: ZeroIntensity
comments.body: I don't, but you can just CTRL+F `iter` in the memoryview tests and you'll probably find it :)

comments.author: ritvikpasham
comments.body: It look like there was a previous test case that tests CHECK_RELEASED, so I just added a check to the iter method there.

comments.author: vstinner
comments.body: @sobolevn: I let you review the updated PR. You can merge it if it looks good to you.

comments.author: ZeroIntensity
comments.body: I think so, it's a bugfix.
